top_signals.title: Fitch Ratings Revises Global Shipping Outlook to Neutral on War-Led Tanker Rate Surge
top_signals.summary: Fitch Ratings has revised its 2026 outlook for the global shipping sector to ‘neutral’ from ‘deteriorating’, reflecting a war-driven increase in demand for alternative suppliers and routes, primarily for oil and oil products. This has led to higher tonne-mile demand and a sharp increase tanker shipping rates.  Container shipping has also benefitted moderately from higher rates, ...

top_signals.title: BIMCO adopts biofuel clause to support transition to low-carbon fuels
top_signals.summary: BIMCO has adopted a Biofuel Clause for Time Charter Parties to address legal and operational challenges as the use of low-carbon fuels in shipping increases. As the shipping industry decarbonises, regulatory frameworks reshape companies’ fuel strategies. top_signals.title: Personal Income and Outlays, April 2026
top_signals.summary: Personal income decreased less than $0.1 billion (less than 0.1 percent at a monthly rate) in April, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA). Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-decreased $19.9 billion (0.1 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $111.1 billion (0.5 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $114.0 billion in April. Personal saving was $611.7 billion in April, and the personal saving rate-personal saving as a percentage of DPI-was 2.6 percent. Full Text
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Baltic Dry Index Falls for 10th Day
top_signals.summary: The Baltic Exchange’s dry bulk freight index, which monitors rates for ships carrying dry bulk commodities, was down for the tenth successive session on Thursday, falling about 1.5% to a new low since May 1st at 2,729 points. alerts.title: GDP (Second Estimate) and Corporate Profits, 1st Quarter 2026
alerts.summary: Real gross domestic product (GDP) increased at an annual rate of 1.6 percent in the first quarter of 2026 (January, February, and March), according to the second estimate released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. In the fourth quarter of 2025, real GDP increased 0.5 percent. The contributors to the increase in real GDP in the first quarter were exports, investment, consumer spending, and government spending. Imports, which are a subtraction in the calculation of GDP, increased. Full Text

alerts.title: South Asian demand keeps pushing coal flows higher
alerts.summary: Global seaborne coal flows reached 119mt in May 2026, up 5% y/y — the first month of consecutive y/y growth in 2026 so far, and with an accelerating growth rate compared with April’s revised figure of 4%. Chinese seaborne coal imports fell by 6% y/y but did rise m/m to reach 30mt. Much of this ...

alerts.title: DP World Launches First Global Seagrass Restoration Initiative In South Korea
alerts.summary: DP World has launched its first global seagrass restoration initiative on Geoje Island in South Korea, supporting efforts to restore 2.4 hectares of coastal marine habitat over the next four years and enhancing marine biodiversity. The initiative in South Korea was formalised through a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) between DP World, Korea Green Foundation and ...
